title: Bound direct Chronik outbox import scaling and receipt integrity
goal: Measure and bound the direct Grabowski-to-Chronik importer as source files and ledger history grow, replace repeated whole-ledger reconciliation only when evidence justifies it, and preserve fail-closed recovery after receipt or data loss.

acceptance.assertion: A deterministic benchmark covers representative and stressed source-file and event-count tiers, reports elapsed time, target scans, memory and written versus skipped events, and records machine context.
The task defines an explicit performance threshold and does not add an index, database or cache unless the current implementation breaches that threshold reproducibly.
If optimization is required, one batch reconciles existing event IDs without rescanning the complete target ledger once per source file.
Import receipts remain evidence bound to source path and SHA-256 but never replace verification against the actual Chronik store.
Tests prove recovery after target-ledger loss, receipt loss, stale receipt, appended source file, duplicate event and incomplete JSONL tail without partial success claims.
The deployed two-minute timer has a measured worst-case runtime and overlap policy that prevents concurrent importers or unbounded backlog.
Chronik tests, local validation, GitHub CI and a live repeated import pass at the exact reviewed head and merged release.

rollback.trigger: The grouped importer weakens event-id conflict detection, store verification, append-only behavior, truthful partial-failure reporting or replay recovery.
rollback.procedure: Atomically repoint /home/alex/.local/lib/chronik/current to /home/alex/.local/lib/chronik/releases/2fc65efe06cd23baa2a2bdef39f5f099c8a5540b, reload user units, run one manual import and retain PR #222, its benchmark and runtime receipt as historical evidence.

conditions: current measured runtime remains comfortably below the defined timer and operator latency thresholds
the proposed optimization introduces a second truth store without a corruption and rebuild contract
recovery from missing target data would trust receipts instead of replaying source events
concurrent importer exclusion cannot be proven

current_measurement.assessment: Current volume is operationally acceptable; repeated full-ledger scans may become material only at substantially larger history.

does_not_establish: need for SQLite or another database
permission to reduce validation or deduplication
future performance without a fresh benchmark
metadata.outcome: The direct Grabowski outbox importer now validates and reconciles every valid source batch against the authoritative Chronik ledger with one target scan, keeps receipts nonauthoritative and reconstructible, preserves truthful ledger statistics after receipt-write failure, and remains far inside the two-minute timer budget without a persistent index.

benchmark.index_decision: Deferred until a later reproducible benchmark violates the documented runtime or scan thresholds; no second consistency domain is justified now.

deployment.import_service: inactive/dead after successful one-shot, exit status 0

review.separate_follow_up: CHRONIK-ECOSYSTEM-CLOSEOUT-V1-T008 records the pre-existing low-level partial JSONL append rollback boundary; T006 does not claim to solve it.

acceptance_evidence.benchmark-contract: tools/benchmark_outbox_import.py deterministically records machine context, elapsed time, peak memory, scans, scanned records and written/skipped counts for both tiers and repeated imports.
acceptance_evidence.threshold-defined-before-optimization: The 5 s, 30 s, one-scan and 60 s bounds were recorded before the optimization; the baseline violated them and the final implementation passes without an index.
acceptance_evidence.single-batch-reconciliation: The projected 1000-file batch performs exactly one target-ledger scan instead of 1000.
acceptance_evidence.receipt-not-authority: Every repeat import scans the actual ledger even when the source and receipt are unchanged; missing or stale receipts are rebuilt only after real ledger verification.
acceptance_evidence.loss-recovery-proof: Tests pass for ledger loss, receipt loss, stale receipt, appended source, duplicate/conflicting event IDs, incomplete JSONL tails and receipt ENOSPC after successful ledger append.
acceptance_evidence.timer-budget: The measured projected repeat is 2.289956 s against a 60 s per-run budget and a two-minute systemd timer; the one-shot service excludes parallel starts.
acceptance_evidence.full-regression-proof: 305 tests, local validation, exact-head GitHub CI, merge-blob equality, versioned deploy and two live imports all passed.
